# pfsense_firewall_aliases (Data Source)



## Example Usage

```terraform
# Read every firewall alias defined on the pfSense box.
#
# The data source takes no arguments — it returns the full alias list in
# `data`, so filtering happens in Terraform expressions.

data "pfsense_firewall_aliases" "all" {}

output "alias_names" {
  value = [for alias in data.pfsense_firewall_aliases.all.data : alias.name]
}

# Pick one alias out of the list by name and reuse its entries elsewhere.
output "web_ports" {
  value = one([
    for alias in data.pfsense_firewall_aliases.all.data :
    alias.address if alias.name == "web_ports"
  ])
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `data` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data))

<a id="nestedatt--data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data`

Read-Only:

- `address` (List of String) Sets the host, network or port entries for the alias. When `type` is set to `host`, each
                entry must be a valid IP address or FQDN. When `type` is set to `network`, each entry must be a valid
                network CIDR or FQDN. When `type` is set to `port`, each entry must be a valid port or port range. You
                may also specify an existing alias's `name` as an entry to created nested aliases.<br>
- `descr` (String) Sets a description to help specify the purpose or contents of the alias.<br>
- `detail` (List of String) Sets descriptions for each alias `address`. Values must match the order of the `address` 
                value it relates to. For example, the first value specified here is the description for the first
                value specified in the `address` field. This value cannot contain <br>
- `name` (String) Sets the name for the alias. This name must be unique from all other aliases.<br>
- `type` (String) Sets the type of alias this object will be. This directly impacts what values can be
                specified in the `address` field.<br>
